proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`, after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.

Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 0`, after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.

Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 0`, after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.

Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation == 0`, after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.

analyses.result_summary: Linear model `dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated an adjusted `braf_v600e` coefficient of -0.5549 dependency-score units (z=-111.4). The unadjusted subgroup mean was -0.8047 for `braf_v600e == 1` versus -0.2495 for other lines.

Linear model `dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated an adjusted `egfr_amplification` coefficient of -0.3483 (z=-59.8). The unadjusted subgroup mean was -0.6010 versus -0.2524.

Linear model `dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated an adjusted `erbb2_amplification` coefficient of -0.4323 (z=-81.9). The unadjusted subgroup mean was -0.6856 versus -0.2535.

Linear model `dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated an adjusted `kras_mutation` coefficient of -0.3072 (z=-83.4). The unadjusted subgroup mean was -0.5594 versus -0.2520.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss == 0`, after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
Fast-growing cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Higher `cas9_activity_score` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores, consistent with a screen-quality component in this outcome.

analyses.result_summary: Linear model `dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated an adjusted `nf1_loss` coefficient of -0.0953 (z=-17.6). The unadjusted `nf1_loss == 1` mean was -0.3527 versus -0.2572.
Welch comparison for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` found mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 versus -0.2447 in the rest, effect -0.0369 (z=-10.2).
Linear model `dependency_TMED10 ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated a `cas9_activity_score` coefficient of -0.1036 dependency-score units per one-unit increase (z=-8.0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines below that threshold.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification == 0`, but this may be explained by continuous `copy_number_8q24`.

analyses.result_summary: Welch comparison for `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` found mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3440 versus -0.2452 in the rest, effect -0.0988 (z=-27.2).
Unadjusted `myc_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 versus -0.2523, effect -0.1091 (z=-25.5), supporting a marginal association.

In `dependency_RPL11 ~ myc_amplification + copy_number_8q24 + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score`, `copy_number_8q24` remained strongly negative (beta=-0.1042, z=-23.4), while `myc_amplification` was not independently negative (beta=0.0101, p=0.12).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
Cell lines with both high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` and fast growth `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have especially negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

analyses.result_summary: Linear model `dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated a `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` slope of -0.0156 per log2 TPM (z=-10.7).
The subgroup `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669 AND growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 versus -0.3297 in the rest, effect -0.0595 (z=-9.5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Fast-growing cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Higher `cas9_activity_score` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.

analyses.result_summary: Welch comparison for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` found mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 versus -0.3928 in the rest, effect -0.1043 (z=-28.9).
Linear model `dependency_POLR2A ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated a growth-rate coefficient of -0.3046 per doubling/day (z=-39.1).
In the same model, `cas9_activity_score` had coefficient -0.1029 (z=-7.9), indicating a smaller but significant screen-quality association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation == 0`.
Cell lines with high `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines below that threshold.

analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal"` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 versus -0.2524 in the rest, effect -0.4680 (z=-53.3).
`apc_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 versus -0.2526 in the rest, effect -0.2921 (z=-44.7).

`wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.4694 versus -0.2526 in the rest, effect -0.2168 (z=-40.1). A continuous slope test also supported WNT activity (slope=-0.1056, z=-52.4).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

Within the colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT context, `smad4_loss == 0` enables the strongest `dependency_KIF18A` effect; the complete subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 0` has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

Within the colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT context, `smad4_loss == 1` suppresses the `dependency_KIF18A` effect, so the subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 1` is less negative than the SMAD4-intact subgroup and is not more negative than the rest of the dataset.

analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1119 versus -0.2530 in the rest, effect -0.8589 (z=-79.1).

The complete subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 0` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 versus -0.2530 in all other lines, effect -1.0460 (z=-111.1).

The otherwise matched `smad4_loss == 1` subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.2557 versus -0.3074 in all other lines, effect +0.0517 (z=3.7, p=2.35e-4), showing suppression rather than stronger dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT cell lines, high `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559` marks a subgroup with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than the rest of the dataset.

The subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559 AND smad4_loss == 0` has very negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores, but ASCL2 high expression is mainly a marker rather than a required predicate beyond SMAD4 intact status.

analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1414 versus -0.2750 in the rest, effect -0.8664 (z=-62.2).

Adding `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm >= 3.559` to the SMAD4-intact complete subgroup gave mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.3320 versus -0.2749 in the rest, effect -1.0572 (z=-92.1), only slightly stronger than the SMAD4-intact complete subgroup effect of -1.0460.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` remains associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
`copy_number_8q24` remains associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` after accounting for `myc_amplification`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, and `cas9_activity_score`.
`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` remains associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` after accounting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model `dependency_KIF18A ~ I(colorectal AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819) + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated the subgroup coefficient -0.8596 (z=-125.3).

Adjusted model `dependency_RPL11 ~ myc_amplification + copy_number_8q24 + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated `copy_number_8q24` beta -0.1042 (z=-23.4), while `myc_amplification` was not independently negative.
Adjusted model `dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score` estimated `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` beta -0.0156 per log2 TPM (z=-10.7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify `braf_v600e == 1` as the best-supported subgroup with more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify `egfr_amplification == 1` as the best-supported subgroup with more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify `erbb2_amplification == 1` as the best-supported subgroup with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify `kras_mutation == 1` as the best-supported subgroup with more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify `nf1_loss == 1` as the best-supported molecular subgroup with more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search will identify the complete subgroup `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 0` as the strongest subgroup with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores.

A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will find no strong molecularly specific `dependency_TMED10` subgroup; the main reproducible negative association will be fast growth `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` combined with fast growth as the strongest subgroup with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.
A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify fast growth `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` as the strongest subgroup with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores.

A systematic single-feature and pairwise subgroup search will identify high 8q24 dosage, especially `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`, as the best-supported subgroup with more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores.

analyses.result_summary: The systematic heterogeneity screen selected `braf_v600e == 1` as the dominant `dependency_BRAF` subgroup: mean -0.8047 versus -0.2495, effect -0.5552 (z=-111.1). Pairwise refinements did not change the interpretation.
The systematic screen selected `egfr_amplification == 1` as the dominant `dependency_EGFR` subgroup: mean -0.6010 versus -0.2524, effect -0.3486 (z=-60.3).
The systematic screen selected `erbb2_amplification == 1` as the dominant `dependency_ERBB2` subgroup: mean -0.6856 versus -0.2535, effect -0.4321 (z=-81.7).
The systematic screen selected `kras_mutation == 1` as the dominant `dependency_KRAS` subgroup: mean -0.5594 versus -0.2520, effect -0.3075 (z=-83.3).
The systematic screen selected `nf1_loss == 1` as the dominant molecular `dependency_RIT1` subgroup: mean -0.3527 versus -0.2572, effect -0.0955 (z=-17.7).

The systematic search found the strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup at `lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 0`: mean -1.2990 versus -0.2530, effect -1.0460 (z=-111.1). The matched `smad4_loss == 1` subgroup was not negative, confirming SMAD4 loss suppresses the dependency.

The systematic screen found no strong molecularly specific `dependency_TMED10` subgroup. The strongest reproducible negative association was `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`: mean -0.2816 versus -0.2447, effect -0.0369 (z=-10.2), with `cas9_activity_score` also significant.

The strongest pairwise `dependency_DHX9` subgroup was `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669 AND growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`: mean -0.3892 versus -0.3297, effect -0.0595 (z=-9.5). Continuous MYC expression remained significant in an adjusted model.
The systematic screen selected `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` as the dominant `dependency_POLR2A` subgroup: mean -0.4972 versus -0.3928, effect -0.1043 (z=-28.9).

The systematic screen supported high 8q24 dosage for `dependency_RPL11`: `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` had mean -0.3440 versus -0.2452, effect -0.0988 (z=-27.2). `myc_amplification` was a strong marginal marker but not independent after continuous 8q24 copy number was included.
